verdict: The carpentry occupation has a moderate AI displacement risk, with some tasks being automatable but also requiring human judgment and creativity.

reasoning_steps.title: AI capability gap
reasoning_steps.body: The carpentry occupation has a significant AI capability gap, with tasks like measuring and marking cutting lines, assembling frameworks, and shaping materials being highly automatable.

reasoning_steps.title: Physical dexterity
reasoning_steps.body: However, the physical demands of the job, such as working at heights and handling heavy materials, make it difficult for AI to replace human carpenters.

adjacent_careers.name: Cabinetmaker
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both carpenters and cabinetmakers work with wood and other materials, but cabinetmakers focus more on furniture making and finishing.

adjacent_careers.name: Construction Supervisor
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Construction supervisors oversee construction projects, including carpentry tasks, and require human judgment and decision-making skills.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered tool for measuring and marking cutting lines
recent_developments.impact: This tool could automate some tasks in carpentry, but human judgment and creativity are still required.

recent_developments.headline: Hands-on trades remain among the least automated
recent_developments.impact: Physical, in-situ work captures very little AI use today; trades have the lowest exposure of any major occupation group.

quotes.quote: We do not have enough plumbers, electricians, or HVAC techs. AI cannot fix a leaking pipe. The shortage in the trades is only going to widen.
quotes.speaker: Mike Rowe
quotes.title: Host of Dirty Jobs; founder, mikeroweWORKS Foundation

quotes.quote: The tasks most exposed to large language models are not physical. Plumbers, electricians, and construction workers have the lowest exposure of any major occupation group.
quotes.speaker: Tyna Eloundou and Pamela Mishkin
quotes.title: Researchers, OpenAI
quotes.source: GPTs are GPTs: An Early Look at the Labor Market Impact Potential of LLMs

quotes.quote: The world is short on skilled tradespeople and we have spent two generations telling kids the only path is a four-year degree. AI is now coming for the desk jobs first.
quotes.speaker: Mark Cuban
quotes.title: Investor; co-founder, Cost Plus Drugs
